channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Pro-Iranian Hacking Group Claims Credit for Hack of FBI Director Kash Patel’s Personal Account
item.description: The group that it was making available for download emails and other documents from Patel’s account. The post Pro-Iranian Hacking Group Claims Credit for Hack of FBI Director Kash Patel’s Personal Account appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Cloudflare Pages API is seeing increased error rate
item.description: Mar 27, 15:39 UTCResolved - Cloudflare has identified and resolved an issue impacting the Cloudflare Pages API, Pages Builds and Workers Builds.Customers may have seen impact from 27th March 15:00 UTC to 15:29 UTCDeployed Workers and Pages projects are unaffected

item.title: ClawPhone: Redefining the Smartphone as a Living AI Agent——the first native Android app for OpenClaw
item.description: ClawPhone, the first native Android app for the OpenClaw framework, transforms smartphones into autonomous AI agents capable of cross-app automation, smart home control, and emotionally aware interactions—all without requiring OS root access.

item.title: Open VSX Bug Let Malicious VS Code Extensions Bypass Pre-Publish Security Checks
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a now-patched bug impacting Open VSX's pre-publish scanning pipeline to cause the tool to allow a malicious Microsoft Visual Studio Code (VS Code) extension to pass the vetting process and go live in the registry. "The pipeline had a single...

item.title: The Download: the internet’s best weather app, and why people freeze their brains
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. How a couple of ski bums built the internet’s best weather app  The best snow-forecasting app for...

item.title: AitM Phishing Targets TikTok Business Accounts Using Cloudflare Turnstile Evasion
item.description: Threat actors are using adversary-in-the-middle (AitM) phishing pages to seize control of TikTok for Business accounts in a new campaign, according to a report from Push Security. Business accounts associated with social media platforms are a lucrative target, as they can be weaponized by bad...

item.title: TP-Link Patches High-Severity Router Vulnerabilities
item.description: The security defects could be used to bypass authentication, execute arbitrary commands, and decrypt configuration files. The post TP-Link Patches High-Severity Router Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: We Are At War
item.description: Rising geopolitical tensions are reflected (or in some cases preceded) by cyber operations, while technology itself has become politicized. Let’s admit it: we are in the middle of it.  Introduction: One tech power to rule them all is a thing of the past  The relative safety, peace and...

item.title: RSAC 2026 Conference Announcements Summary (Days 3-4)
item.description: A summary of the announcements made by vendors on the third and fourth days of the RSAC 2026 Conference. The post RSAC 2026 Conference Announcements Summary (Days 3-4) appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Coruna iOS Exploit Kit Likely an Update to Operation Triangulation
item.description: Coruna contains the updated version of a kernel exploit used in Operation Triangulation three years ago. The post Coruna iOS Exploit Kit Likely an Update to Operation Triangulation appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Shenzhen Hello Tech Energy Launches Jackery SolarVault 3 Series in the European Market
item.description: Shenzhen Hello Tech Energy launched the Jackery SolarVault 3 series in Europe, a modular solar storage system starting at 2.5 kWh with AI‑driven energy management that adapts to real‑time electricity prices.

item.title: Bearlyfy Hits Russian Firms with Custom GenieLocker Ransomware
item.description: A pro-Ukrainian group called Bearlyfy has been attributed to more than 70 cyber attacks targeting Russian companies since it first surfaced in the threat landscape in January 2025, with recent attacks leveraging a custom Windows ransomware strain codenamed GenieLocker. "Bearlyfy (also known as...

item.title: iliconCore Technology Launches aiX-apply-4B, Boosting Code Change Efficiency
item.description: SiliconCore Technology unveiled aiX-apply-4B, a lightweight code‑modification model achieving 93.8% accuracy across 20+ programming languages while running on a single consumer‑grade GPU.

item.title: ByteDance’s CapCut Launches Dreamina Seedance 2.0 Across Multiple Global Regions
item.description: ByteDance’s CapCut rolled out its Dreamina Seedance 2.0 AI video model across Africa, South America, the Middle East, and Southeast Asia, enabling 15‑second generations in six aspect ratios.

item.title: Xingyi Technology Secures Tens of Millions in Series A Funding, Focuses on Ego-Centric Embodied Intelligence Data
item.description: Tsinghua‑incubated Xingyi Technology raised tens of millions USD in Series A funding to develop ego‑centric data systems for embodied intelligence, aiming to open‑source up to 10,000 hours of high‑precision datasets this year.

item.title: CISA Flags Critical PTC Vulnerability That Had German Police Mobilized
item.description: Police in Germany physically warned organizations about the critical PTC Windchill vulnerability tracked as CVE-2026-4681. The post CISA Flags Critical PTC Vulnerability That Had German Police Mobilized appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Here’s why some people choose cryonics to store their bodies and brains after death
item.description: This week I reported on some rather unusual research that focuses on the brain of L. Stephen Coles. Coles was a gerontologist who died from pancreatic cancer in 2014. He had spent the latter part of his career specializing in human longevity. And before he died, he decided to have his brain...

item.title: LangChain, LangGraph Flaws Expose Files, Secrets, Databases in Widely Used AI Frameworks
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed three security vulnerabilities impacting LangChain and LangGraph that, if successfully exploited, could expose filesystem data, environment secrets, and conversation history. Both LangChain and LangGraph are open-source frameworks that are used to build...

item.title: Elevated error rates on Opus 4.6
item.description: Mar 27, 06:59 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: ARC-AGI-3: A New Challenge for Frontier Agentic Intelligence
item.description: arXiv:2603.24621v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce ARC-AGI-3, an interactive benchmark for studying agentic intelligence through novel, abstract, turn-based environments in which agents must explore, infer goals, build internal models of environment dynamics, and plan effective action...

item.title: When Is Collective Intelligence a Lottery? Multi-Agent Scaling Laws for Memetic Drift in LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2603.24676v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-agent systems powered by large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in settings that shape consequential decisions, both directly and indirectly. Yet it remains unclear whether their outcomes reflect collective reasoning, systematic...

item.title: AutoSAM: an Agentic Framework for Automating Input File Generation for the SAM Code with Multi-Modal Retrieval-Augmented Generation
item.description: arXiv:2603.24736v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In the design and safety analysis of advanced reactor systems, constructing input files for system-level thermal-hydraulics codes such as the System Analysis Module (SAM) remains a labor-intensive task. Analysts must extract and reconcile design data...

item.title: Trust as Monitoring: Evolutionary Dynamics of User Trust and AI Developer Behaviour
item.description: arXiv:2603.24742v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AI safety is an increasingly urgent concern as the capabilities and adoption of AI systems grow. Existing evolutionary models of AI governance have primarily examined incentives for safe development and effective regulation, typically representing...

item.title: Formal Semantics for Agentic Tool Protocols: A Process Calculus Approach
item.description: arXiv:2603.24747v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The emergence of large language model agents capable of invoking external tools has created urgent need for formal verification of agent protocols. Two paradigms dominate this space: Schema-Guided Dialogue (SGD), a research framework for zero-shot API...

item.title: DyMRL: Dynamic Multispace Representation Learning for Multimodal Event Forecasting in Knowledge Graph
item.description: arXiv:2603.24636v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Accurate representation of multimodal knowledge is crucial for event forecasting in real-world scenarios. However, existing studies have largely focused on static settings, overlooking the dynamic acquisition and fusion of multimodal knowledge. 1) At...

item.title: How unconstrained machine-learning models learn physical symmetries
item.description: arXiv:2603.24638v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The requirement of generating predictions that exactly fulfill the fundamental symmetry of the corresponding physical quantities has profoundly shaped the development of machine-learning models for physical simulations. In many cases, models are built...

item.title: Experiential Reflective Learning for Self-Improving LLM Agents
item.description: arXiv:2603.24639v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) have enabled the development of autonomous agents capable of complex reasoning and multi-step problem solving. However, these agents struggle to adapt to specialized environments and do not leverage past...

item.title: Learning Mesh-Free Discrete Differential Operators with Self-Supervised Graph Neural Networks
item.description: arXiv:2603.24641v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Mesh-free numerical methods provide flexible discretisations for complex geometries; however, classical meshless discrete differential operators typically trade low computational cost for limited accuracy or high accuracy for substantial per-stencil...

item.title: Physics-Informed Neural Network Digital Twin for Dynamic Tray-Wise Modeling of Distillation Columns under Transient Operating Conditions
item.description: arXiv:2603.24644v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Digital twin technology, when combined with physics-informed machine learning with simulation results of Aspen, offers transformative capabilities for industrial process monitoring, control, and optimization. In this work, the proposed model presents...

item.title: When Consistency Becomes Bias: Interviewer Effects in Semi-Structured Clinical Interviews
item.description: arXiv:2603.24651v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Automatic depression detection from doctor-patient conversations has gained momentum thanks to the availability of public corpora and advances in language modeling. However, interpretability remains limited: strong performance is often reported...

item.title: Demystifying When Pruning Works via Representation Hierarchies
item.description: arXiv:2603.24652v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Network pruning, which removes less important parameters or architectures, is often expected to improve efficiency while preserving performance. However, this expectation does not consistently hold across language tasks: pruned models can perform well...

item.title: Fine-Tuning A Large Language Model for Systematic Review Screening
item.description: arXiv:2603.24767v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Systematic reviews traditionally have taken considerable amounts of human time and energy to complete, in part due to the extensive number of titles and abstracts that must be reviewed for potential inclusion. Recently, researchers have begun to...

item.title: Evaluating Fine-Tuned LLM Model For Medical Transcription With Small Low-Resource Languages Validated Dataset
item.description: arXiv:2603.24772v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Clinical documentation is a critical factor for patient safety, diagnosis, and continuity of care. The administrative burden of EHRs is a significant factor in physician burnout. This is a critical issue for low-resource languages, including Finnish....

item.title: Enhancing Structured Meaning Representations with Aspect Classification
item.description: arXiv:2603.24797v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: To fully capture the meaning of a sentence, semantic representations should encode aspect, which describes the internal temporal structure of events. In graph-based meaning representation frameworks such as Uniform Meaning Representations (UMR),...

item.title: [AINews] Everything is CLI
item.description: a quiet day lets us reflect on the growing trend of CLIs for ~everything~ agents

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6
item.description: Mar 27, 00:49 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 26, 23:06 UTCIdentified - We have identified an issue resulting in a low rate of errors on requests to Claude Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6, we are continuing to work to fully resolve this issue and will provide additional updates.Mar 26,...

item.title: Increase in 502 errors
item.description: Mar 26, 23:51 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 26, 23:40 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Mar 26, 23:30 UTCIdentified - Cloudflare is aware of reports and has identified an issue causing an increase of 502 errors for a subset of...

item.title: Responses API and Sora Increased Error Rates
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Responses (Operational)

item.title: Elevated errors for MCP calls
item.description: Mar 26, 22:41 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 26, 22:25 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating an issue resulting in elevated errors for some MCP calls. We will provide an update as soon as possible.

item.title: Incorrect IP source information in spectrum HTTP/HTTPS apps
item.description: Mar 26, 19:08 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 26, 18:35 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Mar 26, 18:29 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.Mar 26, 17:08 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is...

item.title: Less Gaussians, Texture More: 4K Feed-Forward Textured Splatting
item.description: Existing feed-forward 3D Gaussian Splatting methods predict pixel-aligned primitives, leading to a quadratic growth in primitive count as resolution increases. This fundamentally limits their scalability, making high-resolution synthesis such as 4K intractable. We introduce LGTM (Less Gaussians,...

item.title: Unleashing Guidance Without Classifiers for Human-Object Interaction Animation
item.description: Generating realistic human-object interaction (HOI) animations remains challenging because it requires jointly modeling dynamic human actions and diverse object geometries. Prior diffusion-based approaches often rely on hand-crafted contact priors or human-imposed kinematic constraints to improve...

item.title: Wan-Weaver: Interleaved Multi-modal Generation via Decoupled Training
item.description: Recent unified models have made unprecedented progress in both understanding and generation. However, while most of them accept multi-modal inputs, they typically produce only single-modality outputs. This challenge of producing interleaved content is mainly due to training data scarcity and the...

item.title: UNIC: Neural Garment Deformation Field for Real-time Clothed Character Animation
item.description: Simulating physically realistic garment deformations is an essential task for virtual immersive experience, which is often achieved by physics simulation methods. However, these methods are typically time-consuming, computationally demanding, and require costly hardware, which is not suitable for...

item.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
item.description: Our latest voice model has improved precision and lower latency to make voice interactions more fluid, natural and precise.

item.title: PAWS: Perception of Articulation in the Wild at Scale from Egocentric Videos
item.description: Articulation perception aims to recover the motion and structure of articulated objects (e.g., drawers and cupboards), and is fundamental to 3D scene understanding in robotics, simulation, and animation. Existing learning-based methods rely heavily on supervised training with high-quality 3D data...

item.title: The Download: a battery pivot to AI, and rewriting math
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Why this battery company is pivoting to AI  Qichao Hu doesn’t mince words about the state of the battery industry. “Almost...

item.title: The snow gods: How a couple of ski bums built the internet’s best weather app
item.description: The best snow-forecasting app for skiers and snowboarders isn’t from any of the federally funded weather services. Nor from any of the big-name brands. It’s an independent app startup that leverages government data, its own AI models, and decades of alpine-life experience to offer better snow (and...

item.title: Are high gas prices good news for EVs? It’s complicated.
item.description: I live in a dense city with plentiful public transportation options and limited parking, so I don’t own a car. I’m often utterly clueless about the current price of gasoline. But as the conflict in Iran has escalated, fossil-fuel prices have been on a roller-coaster, and I’ve started paying...

item.title: Cloudflare Workers AI Issues
item.description: Mar 26, 04:26 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 26, 04:16 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Mar 26, 03:03 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare has identified an issue whereby customers might experience errors from inference requests to...
